query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
(42, 'What is the "Synoptic Problem" and why is it theologically significant for understanding the Gospels?', 'multiple_choice', '["The problem of contradictions that undermine Gospel reliability", "The question of how Matthew, Mark, and Luke relate literarily while maintaining their unique theological perspectives", "The difficulty of harmonizing the Gospels with secular history", "The challenge of translating Greek into modern languages"]', 'The question of how Matthew, Mark, and Luke relate literarily while maintaining their unique theological perspectives', 1),
(42, 'Which Gospel is generally considered the earliest and likely served as a source for the other Synoptic Gospels?', 'multiple_choice', '["Matthew, because it appears first in the New Testament", "Mark, based on early church testimony and literary analysis", "Luke, due to its detailed historical prologue", "All three were written simultaneously and independently"]', 'Mark, based on early church testimony and literary analysis', 2),
(42, 'How do the distinct theological emphases of Matthew, Mark, and Luke complement rather than contradict each other?', 'multiple_choice', '["They present contradictory portraits of Jesus that cannot be reconciled", "Each Gospel highlights different aspects of Jesus'' person and work for different audiences while maintaining historical and theological unity", "Only one Gospel is historically accurate; the others are theological fiction", "The differences prove the Gospels are unreliable"]', 'Each Gospel highlights different aspects of Jesus'' person and work for different audiences while maintaining historical and theological unity', 3),
(42, 'True or False: The Synoptic Gospels present Jesus primarily as a moral teacher rather than as the divine Son of God and Messiah.', 'true_false', '["True", "False"]', 'False', 4),
(42, 'In 2-3 sentences, explain how Matthew''s emphasis on Jesus as the fulfillment of Old Testament prophecy serves his theological purpose and audience.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Matthew wrote primarily to a Jewish-Christian audience, demonstrating that Jesus is the promised Messiah who fulfills the Law and the Prophets (Matthew 5:17). His frequent use of fulfillment formulas ("This was to fulfill what was spoken...") establishes continuity between the Old and New Covenants. This approach bridges Jewish expectations with the reality of Jesus'' person and work, showing that Christianity is the authentic continuation of biblical Judaism.', 5),
(43, 'How does John''s Gospel differ from the Synoptic Gospels in its presentation of Jesus Christ?', 'multiple_choice', '["John contradicts the Synoptics and presents a different Jesus", "John emphasizes Jesus'' divine nature and theological significance more explicitly while complementing the Synoptic portrait", "John is purely theological fiction with no historical value", "John focuses only on Jesus'' humanity, unlike the Synoptics"]', 'John emphasizes Jesus'' divine nature and theological significance more explicitly while complementing the Synoptic portrait', 1),
(43, 'What is the theological significance of the "I AM" statements in John''s Gospel?', 'multiple_choice', '["They are simple self-descriptions with no deeper meaning", "They echo God''s self-revelation to Moses (Exodus 3:14) and assert Jesus'' divine identity", "They prove Jesus was merely a prophet", "They are later additions not original to John''s Gospel"]', 'They echo God''s self-revelation to Moses (Exodus 3:14) and assert Jesus'' divine identity', 2),
(43, 'According to John 20:30-31, what is the stated purpose of John''s Gospel?', 'multiple_choice', '["To provide a complete biography of Jesus'' life", "That readers might believe Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and have life in His name", "To correct errors in the Synoptic Gospels", "To establish church doctrine and practice"]', 'That readers might believe Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and have life in His name', 3),
(43, 'True or False: The prologue of John''s Gospel (John 1:1-18) establishes Jesus'' pre-existence, deity, and incarnation as foundational to understanding His identity and mission.', 'true_false', '["True", "False"]', 'True', 4),
(43, 'In 2-3 sentences, explain how John''s use of "signs" (σημεῖα) functions to reveal Jesus'' glory and identity.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'John selects seven specific miraculous signs that reveal Jesus'' divine power and messianic identity, pointing beyond the physical miracle to spiritual truth (John 2:11). Each sign demonstrates an aspect of Jesus'' glory and invites faith, serving both evidential and symbolic purposes. The signs culminate in Jesus'' resurrection, the ultimate sign validating His claims and mission (John 20:30-31).', 5)
